title: Pungent Turkish Beef Pita-Based Dishes (Iskender Form)
description: A tangy, crunchy monsoon dish featuring filo pastry and bhuna, inspired by Turkish flavors. Serve hot, garnished with parsley and lemon wedges.
instructions: Cut filo pastry sheets into 4 equal pieces.
Layer 2-3 sheets of filo pastry in the bottom of a 9x13 inch baking dish, brushing with melted butter.
Repeat layering process until all filo pastry is used, ending with a layer of butter on top.
In a large pan, cook ground beef over medium-high heat, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Once beef is browned, add chopped onion, minced garlic, and bhuna curry paste. Cook until onion is translucent and fragrant.
Add salt, black pepper, and lemon juice to the pan, stirring to combine. Cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Pour the beef mixture over the top of the filo pastry, spreading it evenly.
Cut pita bread into quarters and pile on top of the beef mixture, creating a stack.
Bake in preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until filo pastry is golden brown and pita bread is crispy.
Remove from oven and garnish with chopped parsley. Serve hot, with lemon wedges on the side.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
